entry.title: how to trigger handler class read method in RAP OData V4 Web API?
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

Good day.

Well, I have a requirement, existing material data coming from third party system to CPI to S/4 HANA MDG and need to create change request and approve it.

So created a RAP service, Table , CDS view , service definition with binding type OData V4 Web API along with behavior definition/implementation In the handler class, defined the methods modify and read. I just want to see if read method is called and data filled. So i would like to know how i can execute/debug the handler class read method.

How can i do the GET call using GW client?

Service URL :  /sap/opu/odata4/sap/xxx_xx_xxx_sb/srvd_a2x/sap/xxxx_xx_xxxx_s/0001/

xxx_xx_xxx_sb -  Service binding         xxxx_xx_xxxx_s  - Service definition

 

 

 

regards

Chandra Kishore.

entry.title: Update custom SuccessFactors MDF field once workflow has been approved or sent back
entry.content:
Hi all

I have a custom MDF field that I would like to update based on the outcome of the workflow (either approved or send back). I have tried to configure business rules for the custom mdf object under "save rules" on the custom mdf object page, but it seems the status gets changed immediately as a new record is created and before the workflow gets approved, even though Pending data is set to "Yes".

I have created a new business rule by choosing Metadata framework > Rules for MDF objects and filling out the fields below:

I have also tried using the different options under purpose, but none seem to give me the correct options I need when structuring my IF statement (e.g. When workflow onsave = approve then status.value = approved etc.) 

Is there any possible workaround/correct solution to achieve this?

entry.title: OData V4 URL Encoding Issue with SAP Web Dispatcher + Proxy (Sales Order Management App)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with OData V4 URL encoding after activating the standard Sales Order Management application in S/4HANA.

Note: An SAP incident has already been created, and in parallel we are reaching out to the community to learn from experts who may have faced a similar issue.

Issue summary

The problem occurs because equal signs (=) in the OData V4 request URL are being URL-encoded (%3D) before the request reaches the S/4HANA ICM.

In the browser, the request URL is correct and the = signs are not encoded:

https://etgwdsp.etgworld.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/c_salesordermanage_srv/srvd_f4/sap/c_orgdivisionvaluehelp/0001;ps='srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va='com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

However, when the request reaches the ICM, the raw HTTP request shows that:

  • = is encoded as %3D

  • %27 is converted back to '

Example from ICM level 3 trace:

GET /sap/opu/odata4/.../0001;ps%3D'srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va%3D'com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

The SAP Gateway expects the “=” characters to remain unencoded, and because of this encoding, the request fails.

Landscape and behavior

Middleware involved

  • SAP Web Dispatcher

  • Corporate HTTP Proxy

Observed behavior

  1. All apps work when the proxy is bypassed (Web Dispatcher active)

  2. All apps work when the Web Dispatcher is bypassed (proxy active)

  3. The issue occurs only when both proxy and Web Dispatcher are active

This strongly indicates that the URL is being modified due to an interaction between the proxy and Web Dispatcher.

Troubleshooting performed

  • All required roles, authorizations, services, and ICF nodes are active

  • Web Dispatcher profile parameters adjusted to prevent URL encoding

  • Web Dispatcher mod file changes attempted for OData V4 handling
    → Issue still persists

As per internal network team analysis:

  • A change may be required in the Web Dispatcher index file

  • Neither the network team nor BASIS team has access to modify it

  • Only SAP can make changes at this level

Questions to the community

  • Has anyone experienced a similar OData V4 URL encoding issue with Web Dispatcher + proxy?

  • Is this a known limitation or defect in SAP Web Dispatcher for OData V4?

  • Are there any supported parameters, SAP Notes, or workarounds to prevent encoding of = in the URL?

  • Should this be handled via ICM settings, Web Dispatcher configuration, or proxy rules?

  • Is there any recommended architectural workaround until SAP provides a fix?

Any guidance, experiences, or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Varsha J S

 

#SAP #S4HANA #ODataV4 #SAPGateway #SAPWebDispatcher #ICM #SAPBasis #SAPFiori #SAPCommunity
@SAP @SAPCommunity @SAPSupport

entry.title: Guidance on best adapter choice for inbound integration to SAP Cloud Private via CPI
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am working on an inbound integration scenario using SAP Cloud Integration.
An external (non-SAP) application will send data through CPI to SAP Cloud Private, mainly for creating and updating business objects.

From a design perspective, I am currently leaning toward OData services because they are standardized, REST-based, support CRUD operations, and are easier for future consumers such as web or mobile applications.

However, I would like to validate this assumption against real project experience and community best practices.

I would appreciate your advice on the following:

  1. In similar inbound scenarios, which adapter is most commonly recommended?

    • OData

    • IDoc

    • RFC/BAPI

    • SOAP

    • other approaches?

  2. Under what conditions would you recommend not using OData, even if the requirement is mainly CRUD?

  3. Looking at performance, monitoring, extensibility, and long-term maintenance, which option tends to be the most sustainable?

  4. If the backend is built using modern paradigms such as RAP/CDS, does that naturally position OData as the primary interface?

I am open to alternative recommendations if other approaches are considered more suitable.

Thank you very much for sharing your experience.

entry.title: Unable to update "Your Reference" (VBKD-IHREZ) using standard api API_CUSTOMER_RETURN extension.
entry.content:
Hello,

The business requirement is to use API_CUSTOMER_RETURN to create return orders in S/4 (Private cloud).  There return orders are created referencing multiple invoices in the satellite system.  Reference Invoice Numbers should be stored in "Your Reference" (VBKD-IHREZ) field at the header and line item levels in S/4.  VBKD-IHREZ is not exposed in the standard API.

Extending CDS views A_CustomerReturn and A_CustomerReturnItem with CorrespncExternalReference field only reads VBKD-IHREZ in the get operation.

I am unable to save the values with post operation.  Has anybody implemented this?  Please share the details.

Thanks

Lokesh

entry.title: How to extend API_CUSTOMER_RETURN with VBKD-IHREZ for CRUD?
entry.content:
The business requirement is to use API_CUSTOMER_RETURN to create return orders in S/4 (Private cloud). There return orders are created referencing multiple invoices in the satellite system. Reference Invoice Numbers should be stored in "Your Reference" (VBKD-IHREZ) field at the header and line item levels in S/4. Standard API doesn't expose VBKD-IHREZ.


Extending CDS views A_CustomerReturn and A_CustomerReturnItem with CorrespncExternalReference field only reads VBKD-IHREZ in the get operation.

I am unable to save the values with post operation. Has anybody implemented this?  Please share the details.

 

Thanks

Lokesh

entry.title: POST Call for UI_MAINTWORKREQUESTOVW_V2 with C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP Entity gives Forbidden
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to test POST call for UI_MAINTWORKREQUESTOVW_V2 with C_MaintWorkRequestOverviewTP and to_EventPrio Navigation for Priority creation but its giving Dump and Forbidden error on Gateway client but same works as expected on "Screen Maintenance Request" application.

We are having Maintenance notification created and when we Edit it and tries to add Priorities POST call works and gives created response in Network tab.
Same when I trying on Gateway client its giving Forbidden and Short dump below

Can someone help me to find what exactly I am doing wrong?

Thank you,

Ankita

entry.title: Accessing fields in RAP app controller using OData (V4)
entry.content:
Hi Developers,

Sorry if this is an easy one but I have not done this before so I am a bit stuck.

I have made a RAP application using Eclipse and Business Application Studio. I am generating a Fiori List Report using OData V4 and the data is passing through fine.

I now want to customise the header using an extension point. I have also completed this with static text as seen below.

 


The problem is, I want to be able to display the data that is in the list but in the header instead where I can perform calculations.

For example let's say the list shows multiple entries of employees and a field is the number of days left of annual leave. In the header I want to be able to calculate and display the total number of days of annual leave remaining for all employees.

I have currently setup a controller and I'm attempting to try and get the data in section from the hook

onAfterRendering. I have explored developer tools but I cant seem to find anything.
 
If you know how to do this, please add as much detail as you can.
 
Thank you very much!
OData 
#

entry.title: Clarification on SAP S/4HANA APIs for Customer, Billing, and Financial History Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working on integrating an external web application with SAP S/4HANA and would appreciate some guidance on selecting the appropriate APIs/services.

Application Context:
My application functions as a customer management portal. In the existing system (Oracle-based), we display and manage:

  • Customer data

  • Bill / Invoice details

  • Bill / Invoice history

  • Financial account (FA) history

  • To-Do management (Add / Update / Delete operations)

We now need to implement similar functionality using SAP S/4HANA.

Clarifications Needed:

  1. Customer Data
    Which APIs are recommended for retrieving and managing customer master data?

  2. Billing / Invoice Details
    Which standard APIs should be used to fetch:

    • Invoice / billing details

    • Invoice history / open items

  3. Financial Account / History
    For financial/account-related history (balances, transactions, etc.), which APIs or services are appropriate?

  4. CRUD Operations
    For operations such as Add / Update / Delete (e.g., To-Dos or related entities), should we rely on:

    • Standard OData APIs

    • CDS-based services

    • Custom APIs

  5. Best Practice Approach
    From an architectural perspective, what is the recommended approach:

    • Standard SAP APIs (API Hub)

    • Custom CDS Views + OData

    • RAP / BTP-based services

Any pointers to relevant APIs, documentation, or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Kavinkumar

entry.title: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record but does NOT trigger workflow
entry.content:
Hi everyone

I'm trying to create a new record in a custom MDF object (cust_mymdf) via the OData v2 API using a POST to the /upsert endpoint, with the goal of triggering the configured approval workflow.

What I'm trying to achieve:

  • Use the OData API to insert a termination request record as pending data.
  • Have the system's standard MDF workflow (configured on the object with a workflow group for approval) automatically trigger upon creation via API, so an approver receives a notification and can review/approve/reject.

Setup details:

  • Custom MDF object: cust_mymdf (effective-dated, externalCode as key field).
  • Pending Data = Yes on the object definition.
  • Workflow is configured directly on the object (have also tried business rule on save).
  • API user:   (non-admin/technical user, no "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission granted in RBP).
  • Authentication: Basic Auth (username (myuser@companyid) + password).

API call (via Postman):

  • Method: POST
  • URL:https://api55preview.sapsf.eu/odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true

Result:

  • HTTP 200 OK
  • Response body:
{
  "d": [
    {
      "key": "cust_mymdf/effectiveStartDate=2026-02-19T00:00:00.000Z,cust_mymdf/externalCode=19",
      "status": "OK",
      "editStatus": "UPSERTED",
      "message": null,
      "index": 0,
      "httpCode": 200,
      "inlineResults": null
    }
  ]
}​
  • The record appears in Manage Data
  • No workflow is triggered — no approval request is created, no notification sent, nothing appears in Manage Workflow Requests or the approver's inbox.

    What I've already checked / tried:

    • The parameter ?workflowConfirmed=true is included in the URL (as required per sap documentation to simulate UI confirmation).
    • The API user does not have the "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission.
    • Manual creation via UI (Manage Data → Create new record for cust_MassTerminate) does trigger the workflow correctly (approver sees it).
    • Effective date is future-dated (2026-02-19), but same behavior occurs if I test with current date.
    • Workflow is set to trigger on create.
    • No duplicate pending records on the same key.

      According to SAP KBA 2396714 ("Workflow not Triggered via API or Integration Center") and community blogs (e.g., "Triggering workflows for MDF using OData API"), the combination of:

      • workflowConfirmed=trueand
      • API user without "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission

      Is there any additional paramters that I'm missing? Any help or pointers would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: OData v2 upsert on custom MDF Object creates record but does NOT trigger workflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to create a new record in a custom MDF object (cust_mymdf) via the OData v2 API using a POST to the /upsert endpoint, with the goal of triggering the configured approval workflow.

What I'm trying to achieve:

  • Use the OData API to insert a termination request record as pending data.
  • Have the system's standard MDF workflow (configured on the object with a workflow group for approval) automatically trigger upon creation via API, so an approver receives a notification and can review/approve/reject.

Setup details:

  • Custom MDF object: cust_mymdf (effective-dated, externalCode as key field).
  • Pending Data = Yes on the object definition.
  • Workflow is configured directly on the object (have also tried business rule on save).
  • API user:   (non-admin/technical user, no "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission granted in RBP).
  • Authentication: Basic Auth (username (myuser@companyid) + password).

API call (via Postman):

  • Method: POST
  • URL:https://api55preview.sapsf.eu/odata/v2/upsert?workflowConfirmed=true
  • Body (JSON):
{
  "__metadata": {
    "uri": "cust_mymdf(effectiveStartDate=datetime'2026-02-19T00:00:00',externalCode='19')",
    "type": "SFOData.cust_mymdf"
  },
  "externalCode": "19",
  "effectiveStartDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "externalName": "TestTerminate19Update",
  "cust_Employee": "B200394",
  "cust_TerminationDate": "/Date(1771459200000)/",
  "cust_OkToRehire": "Y",
  "cust_DeactivatePosition": "N"
}

Result:

  • HTTP 200 OK
  • Response body:
{
  "d": [
    {
      "key": "cust_mymdf/effectiveStartDate=2026-02-19T00:00:00.000Z,cust_mymdf/externalCode=19",
      "status": "OK",
      "editStatus": "UPSERTED",
      "message": null,
      "index": 0,
      "httpCode": 200,
      "inlineResults": null
    }
  ]
}

 

  • The record appears in Manage Data
  • No workflow is triggered — no approval request is created, no notification sent, nothing appears in Manage Workflow Requests or the approver's inbox.

    What I've already checked / tried:

    • The parameter ?workflowConfirmed=true is included in the URL (as required per sap documentation to simulate UI confirmation).
    • The API user does not have the "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission.
    • Manual creation via UI (Manage Data → Create new record for cust_MassTerminate) does trigger the workflow correctly (approver sees it).
    • Effective date is future-dated (2026-02-19), but same behavior occurs if I test with current date.
    • Workflow is set to trigger on create.
    • No duplicate pending records on the same key.

      According to SAP KBA 2396714 ("Workflow not Triggered via API or Integration Center") and community blogs (e.g., "Triggering workflows for MDF using OData API"), the combination of:

      • workflowConfirmed=trueand
      • API user without "Admin access to MDF OData API" permission

      should allow the workflow to trigger via API — but it's not happening in my case. Has anyone successfully triggered a custom MDF workflow via OData upsert in this scenario? Any additional prerequisites, payload tweaks, or known limitations I'm missing? Any help or pointers would be greatly appreciated — thank you!

entry.title: Issue Filtering Empty Fields in OData Analytical Model Calls
entry.content:
Hello,

Over the past few weeks, we have been experiencing issues with OData calls to analytical models when applying filters on empty fields.

We are building the filter, for example, as follows:
ZOFFICE ne ' '

Until recently, this expression worked correctly. However, it is no longer excluding records with empty values (' '). Instead, it returns the full result set, including those records where the field is empty.

Has there been any recent change in how empty values are interpreted or handled in OData filters? We have also tried alternatives such as using null or comparison operators like gt, but none of them have produced the expected behavior.

Thank you,
Jaume

entry.title: CDS-Based OData 2.0 – Power BI Service Returns Status Code 400 (Invalid Credentials) for Production
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are experiencing an issue with a CDS-based OData service (auto-published via @odata.publish: true) when connecting from Power BI Service.

System & Technical Info:

  • OData Version: 2.0

  • Authentication Type: Basic Authentication

  • Same User ID used in both clients

  • User authorizations confirmed identical (Full Access)

Issue Details:

  • Client 300 (Production) – Power BI Service: Fails with “Failed to update data source credentials: The credentials provided for the OData source are invalid. Status Code: 400.”

  • Client 300 – Power BI Desktop: Works without issues

  • Client 180 (Development) – Power BI Service & Desktop: Works without issues

  • OData URLs (Client 180 & 300): Both accessible and responsive

Checks Performed:

  • No authorization errors in SU53

  • No relevant errors in /IWFND/ERROR_LOG

  • Roles and authorizations between clients verified identical

Question:
Has anyone encountered a situation where a CDS-based OData service works in Power BI Desktop, works in Power BI Service for one client, but fails in Power BI Service for another client with Status Code 400?

Which additional checks would you recommend for:

  • CDS OData activation consistency

  • /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE registration

  • SICF node configuration differences

  • Client-specific security or profile parameters

  • Basic Authentication handling in Power BI Service

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Service Definition based on Consumption CDS View (BOPF-Based CDS View)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am curious if it is possible to create a Service Definition based on the BOPF-based consumption CDS View? This would be theoretically an easier way to create and publish OData V2 or V4 services in SAP, but still not replacing all the backbone from the old programming mode for SAP Fiori to ABAP Restful Programming Model (RAP).

I tried this on an SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 system and the service definition and service binding could be created and activated without any problems, but later one when checking the metadata of the new OData V4 service I am receiving some strange issues regarding a quantity field and its unit of measure

Unit code property 'DAIRYDELIVPROPOSALBASEQTY' not found in complex type 'CT_B93A2928B399FBCED4EC7994740'

it is quite strange since for me the related annotations are properly defined in the consumption CDS View.

Is anyone tried this out and maybe could confirm if this is working or not?

Many thanks,
Catalin

entry.title: Access CDS View data through SAP Integration Suite for Power BI / Excel without embedding credential
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to expose data from an SAP CDS View through SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) so that it can be consumed from Power BI and Excel. It is compulsory to use Integration Suite.

The main constraint of the scenario is authentication.

The Power BI and Excel files will be shared with multiple users, so I cannot embed credentials (for example in VBA scripts or connection strings).
Instead, the idea is to expose the CDS data through Integration Suite as a controlled endpoint.

The desired architecture is:

 
Power BI / Excel
↓
SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)
↓
SAP Gateway OData Service (generated from CDS View)
 

The CDS View is already published as an OData service in SAP Gateway, and it works correctly when accessed directly.

What I have tried so far

I tried two different approaches in Integration Suite.

1. OData → OData Integration Flow

First I created an integration flow using OData adapters on both sides.

Configuration:

Sender adapter

OData V2
Operation: Query (GET)

Receiver adapter

 

OData V2
Resource path: <EntitySet>
Query options: ${header.CamelHttpQuery}
Authentication: Basic (credential stored in Integration Suite)

Goal: create a transparent OData proxy.

Result:

  • The iFlow deployed successfully.

  • I was able to retrieve the metadata from the CDS view through this approach.

  • PowerBI detected the Set BUT gave Internal Server Error (Error no500), so I could not access the info (same problem if tried in browser).

2. HTTPS Sender → HTTP Receiver proxy

I then attempted a (supposely) simpler HTTP proxy approach.

Sender:

HTTPS
Address:
/powerbi/cds02/*
 
Integration flow:
HTTPS Sender
↓
Start
↓
Content Modifier
↓
HTTP Receiver

Content Modifier headers:

CamelHttpPath = ${header.CamelHttpPath}
CamelHttpQuery = ${header.CamelHttpQuery}
 

The idea was to dynamically forward the request path and query string.
In SAP Cloud Integration, the CamelHttpQuery header contains the query parameters from the original request URL.

Receiver configuration:

Address: https://<backend>/sap/opu/odata/sap/<SERVICE_NAME>
Authentication: Basic

Result:

  • The iFlow deploys successfully.

  • However every request returns: An internal server error occured: The MPL ID for the failed message is : (number)

So in practice this HTTP proxy approach does not reach the CDS data at all.

Current status

Summary of what I achieved so far:

Approach Result
OData → ODataMetadata reached (column names got), but not data in it (internal server error)
HTTPS → HTTP proxyRequests fail with internal server error

Questions

  1. What is the recommended architecture to expose CDS data through Integration Suite for consumption by Power BI / Excel?

  2. Is using Integration Suite as an OData proxy the correct approach in this scenario?

  3. Are there special considerations for handling $metadata and query parameters when forwarding OData requests through CPI?

  4. Is there a better approach to solve the authentication constraint (no embedded credentials in Excel / Power BI files)?

Any guidance or examples of similar implementations would be greatly appreciated. I could not even find any tutorials or anything (involving Integration Suite), though I guess it is something used in many companies (it is my first time doing this).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Released CDS View for Sales Price Condition Records with Material ID, in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

System : SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

We are building an external OData API in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
Our goal is to merge Sales Pricing data with Inventory (Material Stock)
data into a single API for downstream consumption.

GOAL:
Expose a combined Stock + Sales Price API by joining:
- I_MaterialStock_2 (inventory/stock levels per plant & storage location)
- A Sales Price CDS view (with Material ID as the join key)

PROBLEM:
The only relevant released CDS view we found is I_SlsPrcgConditionRecord
— but it does NOT include the Material / Product field.
Without Material ID, we cannot join pricing data with stock data
at the material level.

ALREADY CHECKED:
- I_SlsPrcgConditionRecord — no Material ID field
- I_SlsPrcgConditionRecordValidity — Material ID not consistently exposed
- SAP API Business Hub (api.sap.com) — no suitable released OData service found
- SAP Help Portal and SAP Notes — no workaround found

DESIRED API DATA MODEL (illustrative):

SELECT
s.Material,
s.Plant,
s.StorageLocation,
s.MatlWrhsStkQtyInMatlBaseUnit AS StockQuantity,
p.ConditionRateValue AS SalesPrice,
p.TransactionCurrency,
p.ConditionValidityEndDate
FROM I_MaterialStock_2 AS s
JOIN <Missing Sales Price CDS> AS p ON s.Material = p.Material

REQUIRED FIELDS from the missing Sales Price CDS view:
- Material / Product (mandatory join key)
- ConditionType (e.g. PR00)
- ConditionRateValue (price amount)
- TransactionCurrency
- SalesOrganization / DistributionChannel (for filtering)
- ConditionValidityStartDate / ConditionValidityEndDate

QUESTIONS:
1. Does a released CDS view exist that exposes Sales Price
Condition Records WITH Material ID?
2. If not, is SAP planning to release one  and when?
3. What is the SAP-recommended approach to join I_MaterialStock_2
with Sales Price data for an external-facing OData API?
4. If a custom approach is needed, is RAP/BTP extensibility
the right pattern within S/4HANA Cloud boundaries?

We have exhausted all self-service options before posting here.
Any guidance from the community or SAP experts is greatly appreciated!

Thank you.

Rudhra

entry.title: Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigates to transient $uid path instead of server draft path,
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in a CAP + Fiori Elements OData V4 draft application

Problem:

 When I press Create in one specific config app (AggrConfigService / Aggregator), the draft creation flow breaks.

The UI navigates/binds to a transient client-side path like: AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)

CAP then returns: 404 Invalid resource path "AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=...)" What is strange is that the POST itself succeeds first.

Observed request/response

1) Create request: POST Aggregator Body: {"AggregatorID":"Test"}

Response: HTTP/1.1 204 No Content location: Aggregator.drafts('Test') 2) Immediately after that, UI5 sends: GET Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)?$select=AggregatorID,AggregatorName,HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity,IsActive,IsActiveEntity&$expand=DraftAdministrativeData($select=DraftIsCreatedByMe,DraftUUID,InProcessByUser) Response: HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found {"error":{"code":"404","message":"Invalid resource path \"AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)\""}}

So the backend seems to create the draft successfully and returns a draft location, but the frontend still tries to read via the transient $uid path instead of switching to the canonical draft path.

Environment:

SAP CAP Node.js - @sap/cds 8.9.8 - SAPUI5 1.142.0 - Fiori Elements OData V4 - Draft-enabled service - Cloud Foundry deployment on BTP

Questions:

1) Has anyone seen Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigate to a transient `$uid` path like this after a successful POST?

2) Is there any known issue/pattern where FE object-page routing or binding keeps the transient context instead of resolving to the canonical draft location returned by CAP?

Thanks a lot.

Best Regards,

Muthu

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori Elements SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  OData SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: Released API for F-07 / FB01 / F-44 equivalent posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently migrating a custom ECC ABAP report (Bank Reconciliation Statement)
to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) framework as
a Managed Business Object with Draft.

In ECC, the report posted FI entries using BDC recordings for the following
transactions:

- F-06 → Incoming Customer Payment
- F-07 → Outgoing Vendor Payment
- FB01 → Manual GL Document Posting
- F-44 → Vendor Special GL Clearing (Indicator K)

Since BDC is NOT permitted in Public Cloud, I have been searching for the
equivalent released APIs.


✅ RESOLVED — F-06:
I found and implemented CCPAPI_CUSTOMER_PAYMENT_SRV (Communication Scenario
SAP_COM_0276) via OData HTTP POST. This works correctly.


❌ STILL BLOCKED — F-07, FB01, F-44:

► F-07 (Outgoing Payment):
- API_PAYMENT_INITIATION_SRV → Scope mismatch. Covers payment orders,
not direct FI document posting
- I_JournalEntryTP EML → Error: "The operation CREATE is not activated
for entity I_JOURNALENTRYTP"

► FB01 (Manual GL Posting):
- Same BAPI blocked as above
- I_JournalEntryTP EML → Same CREATE not activated error
- No generic GL document posting API found on SAP API Business Hub

► F-44 (Vendor Special GL Clearing):
- I_JournalEntryTP EML with SpecialGLIndicator field → Same CREATE
not activated error
- Factoring Clearing SOAP API → FI-CA module only, not applicable for FI-AP
- No AP account clearing API found


My Questions:
─────────────
1. What is the correct released API / RAP Business Object to post an
outgoing payment (F-07 equivalent) in Public Cloud?

2. What is the correct API for posting a manual GL document (FB01 equivalent)
programmatically from a RAP behavior class?

3. What is the correct API for clearing a vendor special GL indicator K item
(F-44 equivalent)?

4. Is I_JournalEntryTP the correct Business Object for all of these? If yes,
why is the CREATE operation not activated and how can it be enabled?

5. Is there a Communication Scenario that needs to be set up for
I_JournalEntryTP to work via EML?


Technical Context:
──────────────────
- Development: RAP Managed BO, ABAP for Cloud (Tier 1)
- Root BO: ZI_FI_BRS_STATEMENT (based on I_JournalEntryItem)
- Action: PostBRSItems inside behavior class zbp_i_fi_brs_statement
- System: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
- Already tried: MODIFY ENTITIES OF I_JournalEntryTP with COMMIT ENTITIES

Any guidance, SAP Notes, KBA references, or working code examples would be
greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: oModel.read is not working - OData V2
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am new on the SAP BTP/BAS world and I am trying to read a oData model v2 but it is not working.

return Controller.extend("XXX", {
    onInit: async function () { 
      try {
        await this._PickedMaterials(); 
      } catch (e) {
        console.error(e);
        MessageBox.error("Not able to select picket items.");
      }
    },

And here is my funcion _Picked Material

  _PickedMaterials: async function () {
debugger;
var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel("C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2_CDS");
oModel.read("/C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2", {
        urlParameters: { "$top": 20 },
        success: function (data) {
          console.log("SUCCESS DATA:", data);
          console.log("RESULTS:", data.results);
          debugger; 
        },
        error: function (err) {
          console.error("READ ERROR:", err);
          debugger; 
        }
      });

The code reaches the first debugger in the function but it does not enter the sucess or the error conditions. 


oModel is correct:

 and on debug mode shows like this:


Then it gets to oModel.read("/C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2", { It just jumps straight away

I have some console.logs in place like 

oModel.setUseBatch(false);

oModel.attachRequestSent((e) => {
        console.log("RequestSent:", e.getParameters()); // url, method, async, etc.
      });

C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2 is maintained in the backend and response is 200
I can also see the Live data for C_EWM_ODOOVPGQ_2_CDS in Service Center in BAS
OData was added using Service Manager
There is a API being used and working in this app

Any idea what is wrong here?

Thanks
Andréa

entry.title: Issue with OData Service /CPD/SC_PROJ_ENGMT_CREATE_UPD_SRV - Error 414: Resource Type limitation
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am encountering a blocking issue while trying to create a Customer Project using the OData service /CPD/SC_PROJ_ENGMT_CREATE_UPD_SRV (ProjectSet entity).

Context
I am sending a deep insert payload to create a project, its work packages, and the associated resource demands. My payload includes three different resource types:

  • 0ACT (TEAMS)
  • 0ADL (Additional Resource)
  • 0EXP (Expenses)

The Problem
The API call fails with the following error message:

Error Code: /CPD/SS_MESSAGES/414 Message: "You can create or update resource demands only for resource type 0ACT."

Questions

  • Is this a standard limitation of the SC_PROJ_ENGMT_CREATE_UPD_SRV service where it only supports labor-based resources (0ACT)?
  • If so, what is the recommended approach or alternative API to include Expenses (0EXP) and Additional Resources (0ADL) during the project creation phase?

 

Any guidance or workaround would be greatly appreciated as this is a critical part of our integration.

Best regards,
